title: Physical insights into single-component organic photovoltaics
authors: Wenzhi Ma, Liming Liu, Jason A. Röhr, Weitang Li, Jun Zhu, Zhigang Shuai, Jun Yan
abstract: Single-component organic photovoltaics (SC-OPVs) offer enhanced morphological stability over traditional bulk-heterojunction systems, yet their efficiencies currently lag. This review provides a systematic analysis and comparison of the fundamental photophysical processes in both systems, aiming to…

title: Ultrasonic testing in battery research and production
authors: Sam Amsterdam, Aamani Ponnekanti, Nicolina Nanni, Andrew G. Hsieh, Robin James, Erik D. Huemiller, Daniel A. Steingart, Wesley Chang
abstract: This review provides an introduction to ultrasonic testing for battery researchers and an overview of current research directions within the field, covering applications ranging from lab-scale experiments to in-line manufacturing quality control. Perspectives are included from both industry and acad…

title: A low-temperature strategy to prepare CuInS2 solar cells with efficiency exceeding 12%
authors: Wenbo Cao, Chao Dong, Dongxing Kou, Chaofan Zheng, Zhiyang Wan, Longhui Lei, Jiajin Kuang, Yang Wang, Faisal Naveed, Liquan Cheng, Mengqi Jin, Zhaoyang Wang, Yingying Dong, Chong Chen, Mingtai Wang
abstract: Efficient CuInS2 solar cells are processed at < 300°C by combining interdigitation of electron-extracting interfaces and sulfur anion-induced gradient phase transformation (S2−-GPT) in an In-rich CuInS2 layer, and an efficiency of 12.28% and open-circuit voltage of 0.83 V are achieved as the so-far…

title: Pushing the performance of inverted CsPbI3 perovskite photovoltaics via grain boundary grooves stitching
authors: Liyan Hu, Chenzhe Xu, Haochun Sun, Hongwei Wang, Linkai Yu, Jinhao Yang, Feiyu Cheng, Zhuo Kang, Yue Zhang
abstract: This study employs HPDA modification to induce oriented 2D HPDAPbI4 phase formation, which effectively stitches CsPbI3 grain boundary grooves and optimizes film quality, enabling inverted PSCs to achieve a high certified PCE of 21.43% and superior stability.

title: Toward near-100% initial Coulombic efficiency of Si anodes through irreducible solid electrolyte-induced dynamic interphases
authors: Wenxuan Zhao, Zhu Cheng, Victor Vanpeene, Anastasia K. Lavrinenko, Alexandros Vasileiadis, Moumita Rana, Meng-fu Tu, Lars Bannenberg, Victor Landgraf, Sandrine Lyonnard, Yaolin Xu, Lide Yao, Swapna Ganapathy, Marnix Wagemaker
abstract: Silicon anodes promise high energy density for all-solid-state batteries (ASSBs) but are limited by severe lithium loss during the first cycle. This work reveals how controlled interfacial reactions enabled by an irreducible solid electrolyte Li2.45S0.55N0.45 can replenish lithium, enhance initial C…

title: Origami-structured triboelectric nanogenerator for energy and information co-harvesting from a single hand tap
authors: Yawei Wang, Yaozi Zheng, Yizhou Li, Yi Wu, Hao Tang, Minyi Xu, Quanke Su, Guobiao Hu
abstract: Driven by the rapid growth of IoT and wearable technologies, the demand for battery-free, self-powered nodes capable of autonomous sensing and wireless data transmission has become increasingly urgent. This work introduces an origami-inspired triboelectric nanogenerator that is capable of energy and…

title: A fast-charging aluminum-sulfurized polyacrylonitrile battery enabled by electrochemical activation in molten salts
authors: Xiaolin Wu, Yaqi Zhao, Yu Wang, Zhitong Xiao, Junling Huang, Hao Zhang, Yongfeng Jia, Lujun Zhu, Meng Zhang, Xin Tong, Lingzhi Du, Xuanpeng Wang, Jiashen Meng, Quanquan Pang
abstract: This study presents a fast-charging molten salt Al-SPAN battery. Crucially, the electrochemical activation behavior of the SPAN cathode during the first discharge is investigated. The formed Al–N4 coordination structure exhibits dual functionality: (1) residual Al3+ in the Al–N4 configuration reduce…

title: Rapid and non-destructive diagnosis of lithium-ion batteries via quantification of anode spatial degradation
authors: Inwoo Kim, Seongha An, Hyunjae Kim, Jaewook Lee, Hobyung Chae, Wanchuck Woo, Jang Wook Choi
abstract: This study establishes the spatial distribution of anode degradation as a diagnostic descriptor of the health state of commercial lithium-ion batteries and captures it non-invasively using pulse-induced overvoltage relaxation. The diagnostic signal closely reflects degradation behavior, as validated…

title: Suppressing defects in kesterite solar cells via balanced phase evolution to enable 15.1% certified record efficiency
authors: Menghan Jiao, Bowen Zhang, Jinlin Wang, Xiao Xu, Tan Guo, Shudan Chen, Jingchen Wang, Yuan Li, Yiming Li, Huijue Wu, Jiangjian Shi, Yanhong Luo, Dongmei Li, Qingbo Meng
abstract: Kesterite (CZTSSe) solar cells use earth-abundant, non-toxic elements but their efficiency is limited by complex defects. We show that an imbalance between intermediate phases during selenization triggers harmful Zn-related defects. Introducing reactive Na-Sex species accelerates Cu2SnSe3 formation…

title: Antipolar-breaking eliciting giant energy storage in antiferroelectric ceramics
authors: Tengfei Hu, Yuanjie Li, Shiguang Yan, Yufan Li, Lifeng Peng, Xiaohui Liu, Fei Cao, Linlin Zhang, Guanglong Ge, Liqiang He, Zhen Liu, Xuefeng Chen, Zhengqian Fu, Fangfang Xu, Genshui Wang
abstract: The antipolar-breaking strategy in PbZrO3-based AFE materials can substantially postpone the phase-transition field and minimize electric hysteresis, thereby markedly enhancing overall energy-storage capabilities. This strategy will accelerate progress of the research and application in AFE-related…

title: Restoration of spent layered cathodes via direct recalcination
authors: Yaqi Jing, Peng Zhao, Jinyun (Jared) Liao, Zhen Wang, Lei Wang, Lu Ma, Dali Yang, Christopher J. Brooks, Chao Yan, Xiaofang Yang, Huolin L. Xin
abstract: Millions of retired lithium-ion batteries present a critical resource and recycling challenge. Direct recycling, which restores spent battery materials instead of breaking them down, offers a promising solution. This study introduces a clean, low-temperature plasma method to remove key impurities th…

title: Reversing the cold curse on Li-ion battery durability
authors: Dongzhen Lyu, Bin Zhang, Zhibin Zhao, Tao Yang, Zhiwei Gao, Enrico Zio, Jiawei Xiang
abstract: This study challenges traditional thermal management strategies by demonstrating that low-temperature environments can significantly extend lithium-ion battery lifespan. Through a long-term field study, we show how cold exposure, despite short-term capacity loss, can enhance battery durability and r…

title: Limits of electrochemical data for predicting battery lifetime and failure
authors: Peter M. Attia, Andrew Weng, William C. Chueh
abstract: This work highlights the limits of using only electrochemical data sources for early prediction of battery lifetime and failure. Electrochemical data is the primary input for battery lifetime prediction studies today, but many key battery failure modes require non-electrochemical data sources to obt…

title: A multiscale fluid dynamics perspective on bubble management in water electrolysis
authors: Darjan Podbevšek, Miguel A. Modestino
abstract: In gas-evolving electrochemical systems, gas bubble dynamics and the corresponding bubble-induced losses are increasingly recognized as a key efficiency bottleneck, particularly when operating at higher current densities. This perspective overviews the bubble-scale physics; examines the cross-discip…

title: Solar reforming of plastics using acid-catalyzed depolymerization
authors: Papa K. Kwarteng, Yongpeng Liu, Chen Han, Shannon A. Bonke, David M. Vahey, Carolina Pulignani, Erwin Reisner
abstract: Acid-catalyzed depolymerization of waste plastics using recycled sulfuric acid from spent lead-acid batteries produces ethylene glycol, which is selectively photoreformed over an acid-stable metal sulfide-carbon nitride (CoMoS2–CNx) photocatalyst under solar irradiation to generate hydrogen and acet…

title: Taming Active Material-Solid Electrolyte Interfaces with Organic Cathode for All-Solid-State Batteries
authors: Fang Hao, Xiaowei Chi, Yanliang Liang, Ye Zhang, Rong Xu, Hua Guo, Tanguy Terlier, Hui Dong, Kejie Zhao, Jun Lou, Yan Yao

title: From pollutant to product: A lattice-engineering strategy for efficient and selective urea electrolysis
authors: Adam J. Lovett, Roby Soni, Thomas S. Miller
abstract: In the Journal of the American Chemical Society, Chen et al. report a lattice-engineered aluminium-doped NiO electrocatalyst that achieved 98.6% N2 selectivity at 1.45 V vs. RHE during electrochemical oxidation of urea. This preview contextualizes this advancement, discusses open challenges, and sug…

title: Hierarchical architectures in medium-entropy thermoelectrics for high performance
authors: Yuxin Sun, Yue Chen
abstract: In recent years, there has been increased research interest in entropy-engineered thermoelectric materials because of their unique microstructures and superior performance. In a recent Journal of the American Chemical Society article, Liu et al. reported a new medium-entropy AgMnSbPbTe4 alloy with o…

title: SEI architecture enables high-rate Li-mediated ammonia synthesis
authors: Shengliang Zhai, Shaofeng Li
abstract: Lithium-mediated ammonia electrosynthesis is often limited by poor control over solid electrolyte interphase (SEI). In a recent issue of Science, Jun Li and co-workers show that a hierarchical SEI decouples Li+ desolvation from diffusion, enabling continuous ammonia electrosynthesis at 100 mA cm−2 a…

title: Redox-mediated battery recycling with electricity generation
authors: Tianchen Li, Yayuan Liu
abstract: In a recent Nature Communications article, Huang et al. introduced a redox-mediated recycling strategy that recovers critical metals from spent lithium-ion battery cathodes while electrochemically regenerating the acid and base needed for the process. The work reframes battery recycling as an integr…
